title: Claude Code Instructions for dbplot
text: Functions work : - Standard DBI/dbplyr connections (DuckDB, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle) - Spark connections via sparklyr Examples use DuckDB (SQLite) better performance boxplot support. Note: Boxplot functions require database percentile function support. Supported: DuckDB, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Oracle, Spark/Hive supported: SQLite, MySQL < 8.0, MariaDB

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Thank considering contributing dbplot! document provides guidelines instructions contributing project.

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Please note project released Contributor Code Conduct. participating project agree abide terms.

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Bugs tracked GitHub issues. creating bug report, please check existing issues avoid duplicates. reporting bug, please include: clear descriptive title Steps reproduce issue Expected behavior Actual behavior R version relevant package versions Database connection type (applicable) Code examples (preferably using reprex::reprex())

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Enhancement suggestions also tracked GitHub issues. suggesting enhancement, please include: clear descriptive title detailed description proposed enhancement Use cases examples potential drawbacks limitations

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: actively welcome pull requests! Please follow steps: Fork repository create branch main Follow development setup instructions Make changes following code style guidelines Add update tests needed Ensure tests pass Update documentation needed Submit pull request

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: R >= 4.1.0 RStudio (recommended) Git

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Clone fork repository: Install development dependencies: Load package development:
code: git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/dbplot.git cd dbplot # Install devtools if not already installed install.packages("devtools")  # Install package dependencies devtools::install_deps(dependencies = TRUE) devtools::load_all()

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Run tests : Run specific test file:
code: devtools::test() devtools::test_active_file()  # In RStudio with test file open testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-histogram.R")

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Place tests tests/testthat/ prefix test- Use descriptive test names explain tested Use test_that() individual test cases Test expected behavior error conditions Use testthat 3e patterns (context(), use expect_s3_class() instead expect_is()) Example test structure:
code: test_that("dbplot_histogram creates correct number of bins", {   result <- mtcars |>     db_compute_bins(mpg, bins = 10)    expect_s3_class(result, "data.frame")   expect_equal(nrow(result), 10) })  test_that("dbplot_histogram validates bins parameter", {   expect_error(     mtcars |> dbplot_histogram(mpg, bins = -1),     "`bins` must be greater than 0"   ) })

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: aim >70% test coverage. Check coverage :
code: covr::package_coverage() covr::report()  # Opens interactive report

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Follow tidyverse style guide Write clear, readable code meaningful variable names Add comments complex logic, obvious code Keep functions focused single-purpose

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Naming Conventions: - Functions: snake_case (e.g., db_compute_bins, dbplot_histogram) - Variables: snake_case - Arguments: snake_case Spacing: Pipes: - Use native pipe |> (%>%) - One pipe operation per line readability Function Documentation: - Use roxygen2 comments (#') - Include @param, @return, @examples - Keep examples runnable concise Tidy Evaluation: - Use enquo() + !! defusing unquoting - Use enquos() + !!! ... arguments - Use := dynamic column naming - See .github/RLANG_REVIEW.md patterns used package
code: # Good result <- data |>   filter(x > 0) |>   summarise(mean = mean(y))  # Bad result<-data|>filter(x>0)|>summarise(mean=mean(y))

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Format code committing: Check common issues:
code: styler::style_pkg() lintr::lint_package()

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Update roxygen comments adding modifying functions: Regenerate documentation:
code: #' Short description #' #' @description #' Longer description if needed #' #' @param data A table (tbl) #' @param x Variable name #' @param bins Number of bins. Defaults to 30. #' #' @return A data frame with binned data #' #' @examples #' mtcars |> #'   db_compute_bins(mpg) #' #' @export devtools::document()

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Update README.Rmd (README.md directly) Knit README.Rmd regenerate README.md:
code: rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")

text: submitting PR, ensure changes pass checks: checks pass : - 0 errors - 0 warnings - 0 notes (possible)
code: # Run all tests devtools::test()  # Check package devtools::check()  # Check code coverage covr::package_coverage()  # Check URLs urlchecker::url_check()  # Check spelling spelling::spell_check_package()

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Use descriptive branch names: - feature/add-violin-plot - fix/histogram-bin-validation - docs/improve-readme-examples

title: Contributing to dbplot
text: Write clear commit messages:
code: Short summary (50 chars or less)  More detailed explanation if needed. Wrap at 72 characters.  - Bullet points are fine - Reference issues: Fixes #123

text: Update NEWS.md file brief description changes Ensure tests pass package checks clean Update documentation needed Request review maintainers Address review feedback approved, maintainer merge PR

title: Authors and Citation
text: Edgar Ruiz. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ruiz E (2026). dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases. R package version 0.4.0, https://github.com/edgararuiz/dbplot.
code: @Manual{,   title = {dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases},   author = {Edgar Ruiz},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.0},   url = {https://github.com/edgararuiz/dbplot}, }

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: Installation Connecting data source Example Histogram Raster Bar Plot Line plot Boxplot Calculation functions db_bin() Leverages dplyr process calculations plot inside database. package provides helper functions abstract work three levels: Functions output ggplot2 object Functions output data.frame object calculations Functions create formulas calculating bins Histogram Raster plot

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: can install released version CRAN: development version GitHub, using remotes package:
code: install.packages("dbplot") install.packages("remotes") pak::pak("edgararuiz/dbplot")

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: information connect databases, including Hive, please visit https://solutions.posit.co/connections/db/ use Spark, please visit sparklyr official website: https://spark.posit.co

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: functions work standard database connections (via DBI/dbplyr) Spark connections (via sparklyr). local DuckDB database used examples README.
code: library(DBI) library(dplyr)  con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_flights <- copy_to(con, nycflights13::flights, "flights")

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: default dbplot_histogram() creates 30 bin histogram Histogram flight distances default 30 bins Use binwidth fix bin size Histogram flight distances 400-unit bins outputs ggplot2 object, customization can done Customized histogram title theme
code: library(ggplot2)  db_flights |>   dbplot_histogram(distance) db_flights |>   dbplot_histogram(distance, binwidth = 400) db_flights |>   dbplot_histogram(distance, binwidth = 400) +   labs(title = "Flights - Distance traveled") +   theme_bw()

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: visualize two continuous variables, typically resort Scatter plot. However, may practical visualizing millions billions dots representing intersections two variables. Raster plot may better option, concentrates intersections squares easier parse visually. Raster plot basically Histogram. takes two continuous variables creates discrete 2-dimensional bins represented squares plot. determines either number rows inside square processes aggregation, like average. fill argument passed, default calculation count, n() Raster plot scheduled departure arrival times Pass aggregation formula can run inside database Raster plot showing average flight distance time Increase decrease , less, definition. resolution argument controls , defaults 100 Raster plot lower resolution (20x20 grid)
code: db_flights |>   dbplot_raster(sched_dep_time, sched_arr_time) db_flights |>   dbplot_raster(     sched_dep_time,     sched_arr_time,     mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE)     ) db_flights |>   dbplot_raster(     sched_dep_time,     sched_arr_time,     mean(distance, na.rm = TRUE),     resolution = 20     )

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: dbplot_bar() defaults count() value discrete variable Bar plot flight counts origin airport Pass aggregation formula calculated value discrete variable Bar plot average departure delay origin airport
code: db_flights |>   dbplot_bar(origin) db_flights |>   dbplot_bar(origin, avg_delay =  mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: dbplot_line() defaults count() value discrete variable Line plot flight counts month Pass formula operated value discrete variable Line plot average departure delay month
code: db_flights |>   dbplot_line(month) db_flights |>   dbplot_line(month, avg_delay = mean(dep_delay, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: expects discrete variable group , continuous variable calculate percentiles IQR. doesn’t calculate outliers. Boxplot functions require database support percentile/quantile calculations. Supported databases: DuckDB (recommended local examples) - uses quantile() Spark/Hive (via sparklyr) - uses percentile_approx() SQL Server (2012+) - uses PERCENTILE_CONT() PostgreSQL (9.4+) - uses percentile_cont() Oracle (9i+) - uses PERCENTILE_CONT() supported: SQLite, MySQL < 8.0, MariaDB (percentile functions) example using dbplot_boxplot() local data frame: Boxplot flight distances origin airport (local data) Boxplot also works database connections support quantile functions: Boxplot flight distances origin airport (DuckDB)
code: nycflights13::flights |>   dbplot_boxplot(origin, distance) db_flights |>   dbplot_boxplot(origin, distance)

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: customized plot needed, data underpins plots can also accessed: db_compute_bins() - Returns data frame bins count per bin db_compute_count() - Returns data frame count per discrete value db_compute_raster() - Returns data frame results per x/y intersection db_compute_raster2() - Returns db_compute_raster() function plus coordinates x/y boxes db_compute_boxplot() - Returns data frame boxplot calculations data can piped plot Custom histogram arrival delays using db_compute_bins
code: db_flights |>   db_compute_bins(arr_delay)  #> # A tibble: 28 × 2 #>    arr_delay  count #>        <dbl>  <dbl> #>  1      95.1   7890 #>  2     321.     232 #>  3     729.       5 #>  4     548.       6 #>  5     684.       1 #>  6     -40.7 207999 #>  7      NA     9430 #>  8     276.     425 #>  9     457.      23 #> 10     593        6 #> # ℹ 18 more rows db_flights |>   filter(arr_delay < 100 , arr_delay > -50) |>   db_compute_bins(arr_delay) |>   ggplot() +   geom_col(aes(arr_delay, count, fill = count))

title: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases
text: Uses ‘rlang’ build formula needed create bins numeric variable un-evaluated fashion. way, formula can passed inside dplyr verb. Custom histogram arrival delays using db_bin
code: db_bin(var) #> (((max(var, na.rm = TRUE) - min(var, na.rm = TRUE))/30) * ifelse(as.integer(floor((var -  #>     min(var, na.rm = TRUE))/((max(var, na.rm = TRUE) - min(var,  #>     na.rm = TRUE))/30))) == 30, as.integer(floor((var - min(var,  #>     na.rm = TRUE))/((max(var, na.rm = TRUE) - min(var, na.rm = TRUE))/30))) -  #>     1, as.integer(floor((var - min(var, na.rm = TRUE))/((max(var,  #>     na.rm = TRUE) - min(var, na.rm = TRUE))/30))))) + min(var,  #>     na.rm = TRUE) db_flights |>   group_by(x = !! db_bin(arr_delay)) |>   count() #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 2] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.4.4 [edgar@Darwin 25.3.0:R 4.5.2/:memory:] #> # Groups:   x #>         x      n #>     <dbl>  <dbl> #>  1 -40.7  207999 #>  2  NA      9430 #>  3 276.      425 #>  4 457.       23 #>  5 593         6 #>  6   4.53  79784 #>  7 186.     1742 #>  8  95.1    7890 #>  9 321.      232 #> 10 729.        5 #> # ℹ more rows db_flights |>   filter(!is.na(arr_delay)) |>   group_by(x = !! db_bin(arr_delay)) |>   count()|>   collect() |>   ggplot() +   geom_col(aes(x, n)) dbDisconnect(con)

title: Bin formula — db_bin
text: Uses rlang package build formula needed create bins numeric variable unevaluated fashion.  way, formula can passed inside dplyr verb.

title: Bin formula — db_bin
code: db_bin(var, bins = 30, binwidth = NULL)

title: Bin formula — db_bin
text: var Variable name formula bins Number bins. Defaults 30. binwidth Fixed width bin, units data. Overrides bins specified

title: Bin formula — db_bin
text: unevaluated expression (rlang quosure) calculates bin membership   specified variable. expression designed used within dplyr   verbs using `!!` operator.

title: Bin formula — db_bin
code: # \dontrun{ library(DBI) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_mtcars <- copy_to(con, mtcars, "mtcars")  # Important: Always name the field and # prefix the function with `!!` (See Details)  # Uses the default 30 bins db_mtcars |>   group_by(x = !!db_bin(mpg)) |>   count() #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 2] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.4.4 [unknown@Linux 6.14.0-1017-azure:R 4.5.3/:memory:] #> # Groups:   x #>        x     n #>    <dbl> <dbl> #>  1  22.2     2 #>  2  23.7     1 #>  3  15.9     1 #>  4  32.3     1 #>  5  33.1     1 #>  6  18.2     1 #>  7  17.4     2 #>  8  19.0     3 #>  9  15.1     4 #> 10  10.4     2 #> 11  14.3     2 #> 12  26.8     1 #> 13  21.4     3 #> 14  12.8     1 #> 15  25.3     1 #> 16  20.6     2 #> 17  13.5     1 #> 18  16.7     1 #> 19  30.0     2  # Uses binwidth which overrides bins db_mtcars |>   group_by(x = !!db_bin(mpg, binwidth = 10)) |>   count() #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 2] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.4.4 [unknown@Linux 6.14.0-1017-azure:R 4.5.3/:memory:] #> # Groups:   x #>       x     n #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  10.4    18 #> 2  20.4    14  dbDisconnect(con) # }

title: Calculate histogram bins — db_compute_bins
text: Uses dplyr operations create histogram bins. approach, calculations automatically run inside database `data` database sparklyr connection. `class()` tables R : tbl_sql, tbl_dbi, tbl_spark

title: Calculate histogram bins — db_compute_bins
code: db_compute_bins(data, x, bins = 30, binwidth = NULL)

title: Calculate histogram bins — db_compute_bins
text: data table (tbl) x continuous variable bins Number bins. Defaults 30. binwidth Fixed width bin, units data. Overrides bins specified

title: Calculate histogram bins — db_compute_bins
text: ungrouped data.frame two columns: bin values x variable   count observations bin.

title: Calculate histogram bins — db_compute_bins
code: # \dontrun{ library(DBI) library(dplyr) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_mtcars <- copy_to(con, mtcars, "mtcars")  # Returns record count for 30 bins in mpg db_mtcars |>   db_compute_bins(mpg) #> # A tibble: 19 × 2 #>      mpg count #>    <dbl> <dbl> #>  1  22.2     2 #>  2  23.7     1 #>  3  15.9     1 #>  4  32.3     1 #>  5  33.1     1 #>  6  18.2     1 #>  7  17.4     2 #>  8  19.0     3 #>  9  15.1     4 #> 10  10.4     2 #> 11  14.3     2 #> 12  26.8     1 #> 13  20.6     2 #> 14  13.5     1 #> 15  16.7     1 #> 16  30.0     2 #> 17  21.4     3 #> 18  12.8     1 #> 19  25.3     1  # Returns record count for bins of size 10 db_mtcars |>   db_compute_bins(mpg, binwidth = 10) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>     mpg count #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  20.4    14 #> 2  10.4    18  dbDisconnect(con) # }

what: Returns a dataframe with boxplot calculations — db_compute_boxplot
title: Returns a dataframe with boxplot calculations — db_compute_boxplot
text: Uses dplyr operations create boxplot calculations. approach, calculations automatically run inside database `data` database sparklyr connection. `class()` tables R : tbl_sql, tbl_dbi, tbl_spark Requires database support percentile/quantile functions. Confirmed work : DuckDB (recommended local examples) - uses quantile() Spark/Hive (via sparklyr) - uses percentile_approx() SQL Server (2012+) - uses PERCENTILE_CONT() PostgreSQL (9.4+) - uses percentile_cont() Oracle (9i+) - uses PERCENTILE_CONT() work SQLite, MySQL < 8.0, MariaDB (percentile support). Note function supports input tbl already contains grouping variables. can useful creating faceted boxplots.

title: Returns a dataframe with boxplot calculations — db_compute_boxplot
code: db_compute_boxplot(data, x, var, coef = 1.5)

title: Returns a dataframe with boxplot calculations — db_compute_boxplot
text: data table (tbl) can already contain grouping variables x discrete variable group boxplots var continuous variable coef Length whiskers multiple IQR. Defaults 1.5

title: Returns a dataframe with boxplot calculations — db_compute_boxplot
text: ungrouped data.frame boxplot statistics including columns   grouping variable(s), quartiles (lower, middle, upper), whisker bounds   (ymin, ymax), count observations per group.

title: Returns a dataframe with boxplot calculations — db_compute_boxplot
code: # \dontrun{ library(DBI) library(dplyr) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_mtcars <- copy_to(con, mtcars, "mtcars")  db_mtcars |>   db_compute_boxplot(am, mpg) #> # A tibble: 2 × 12 #>      am     n lower middle upper max_raw min_raw   iqr min_iqr max_iqr  ymax #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     0    19  15.0   17.3  19.2    24.4    10.4  6.38    8.57    25.6  24.4 #> 2     1    13  21     22.8  30.4    33.9    15   14.1     6.9     44.5  33.9 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: ymin <dbl>  dbDisconnect(con) # }

what: Aggregates over a discrete field — db_compute_count
title: Aggregates over a discrete field — db_compute_count
text: Uses dplyr operations aggregate data. approach, calculations automatically run inside database `data` database sparklyr connection. `class()` tables R : tbl_sql, tbl_dbi, tbl_spark

title: Aggregates over a discrete field — db_compute_count
code: db_compute_count(data, x, ..., y = n())

title: Aggregates over a discrete field — db_compute_count
text: data table (tbl) x discrete variable ... set named unnamed aggregations y aggregation formula. Defaults count (n)

title: Aggregates over a discrete field — db_compute_count
text: ungrouped data.frame discrete variable one   aggregation columns. first column grouping variable (x), followed   aggregated values.

title: Aggregates over a discrete field — db_compute_count
code: # \dontrun{ library(DBI) library(dplyr) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_mtcars <- copy_to(con, mtcars, "mtcars")  # Returns the row count per am db_mtcars |>   db_compute_count(am) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      am `n()` #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     0    19 #> 2     1    13  # Returns the average mpg per am db_mtcars |>   db_compute_count(am, mean(mpg)) #> Warning: Missing values are always removed in SQL aggregation functions. #> Use `na.rm = TRUE` to silence this warning #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>      am `mean(mpg)` #>   <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1     0        17.1 #> 2     1        24.4  # Returns the average and sum of mpg per am db_mtcars |>   db_compute_count(am, mean(mpg), sum(mpg)) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>      am `mean(mpg)` `sum(mpg)` #>   <dbl>       <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1     0        17.1       326. #> 2     1        24.4       317.  dbDisconnect(con) # }

what: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
title: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
text: visualize two continuous variables, typically resort Scatter plot. However, may practical visualizing millions billions dots representing intersections two variables. Raster plot may better option, concentrates intersections squares easier parse visually. Uses dplyr operations aggregate data. approach, calculations automatically run inside database `data` database sparklyr connection. `class()` tables R : tbl_sql, tbl_dbi, tbl_spark

title: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
code: db_compute_raster(data, x, y, fill = n(), resolution = 100, complete = FALSE)  db_compute_raster2(data, x, y, fill = n(), resolution = 100, complete = FALSE)

title: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
text: data table (tbl) x continuous variable y continuous variable fill aggregation formula. Defaults count (n) resolution number bins created per variable. higher number, records imported source complete Uses tidyr::complete include empty bins. Inserts value 0.

title: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
text: ungrouped data.frame three columns: x variable bins, y variable   bins, aggregated fill values x-y intersection. `db_compute_raster2`: data.frame five columns - x y variable   bins, fill values, additional columns upper bounds bin   (x_2 y_2), useful defining precise tile boundaries.

title: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
text: two considerations using Raster plot database. considerations related size results downloaded database: - number bins requested: higher bins value , data downloaded database. - concentrated data : refers many intersections return value. intersections without value, less data downloaded database.

title: Aggregates intersections of two variables — db_compute_raster
code: # \dontrun{ library(DBI) library(dplyr) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_faithful <- copy_to(con, faithful, "faithful")  # Returns a 100x100 grid of record count of intersections of eruptions and waiting db_faithful |>   db_compute_raster(eruptions, waiting) #> # A tibble: 246 × 3 #>    eruptions waiting `n()` #>        <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> #>  1      3.60    78.5     1 #>  2      4.68    87.5     1 #>  3      3.60    84.9     1 #>  4      1.92    51.0     1 #>  5      4.33    84.9     1 #>  6      1.74    46.7     2 #>  7      4.68    82.8     1 #>  8      1.74    61.6     1 #>  9      1.95    54.7     1 #> 10      4.05    75.9     1 #> # ℹ 236 more rows  # Returns a 50x50 grid of eruption averages of intersections of eruptions and waiting db_faithful |>   db_compute_raster(eruptions, waiting, fill = mean(eruptions), resolution = 50) #> # A tibble: 220 × 3 #>    eruptions waiting `mean(eruptions)` #>        <dbl>   <dbl>             <dbl> #>  1      2.23    61.0              2.28 #>  2      4.68    87.5              4.7  #>  3      1.74    61.0              1.75 #>  4      4.75    83.3              4.8  #>  5      4.47    73.7              4.53 #>  6      1.95    54.7              1.98 #>  7      4.05    75.9              4.08 #>  8      4.4     78.0              4.43 #>  9      4.33    79.0              4.35 #> 10      4.54    83.3              4.57 #> # ℹ 210 more rows  dbDisconnect(con) # }

what: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
title: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
text: Leverages 'dplyr' process calculations plot inside database. package provides helper functions abstract work three levels: outputs 'ggplot', outputs calculations, outputs formula needed calculate bins. dbplot package provides functions create plots using data resides databases remote data sources. leverages dplyr dbplyr push computations database, allowing visualize large datasets without loading entirely R memory.

title: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
text: ## Main Features dbplot provides three levels functionality: 1. **Plot Functions** - Functions output ggplot2 objects:    - [dbplot_histogram()] - Histogram plots    - [dbplot_bar()] - Bar plots discrete variables    - [dbplot_line()] - Line plots discrete variables    - [dbplot_raster()] - Raster/heatmap plots two continuous variables    - [dbplot_boxplot()] - Boxplot grouped continuous data 2. **Computation Functions** - Functions return data frames aggregated data:    - [db_compute_bins()] - Histogram bins counts    - [db_compute_count()] - Counts per discrete value    - [db_compute_raster()] - Aggregated data per x/y intersection    - [db_compute_raster2()] - Raster data box coordinates    - [db_compute_boxplot()] - Boxplot statistics 3. **Utility Functions** - Building blocks custom operations:    - [db_bin()] - Creates binning formulas use dplyr verbs ## Database Compatibility dbplot works database backend supported dplyr/dbplyr, including: - SQLite - PostgreSQL - MySQL/MariaDB - SQL Server - Oracle - Spark (via sparklyr) ## Minimum Requirements - R >= 4.1.0 (native pipe `|>` support) - dplyr >= 1.0.0 - ggplot2 >= 3.3.0 - rlang >= 1.0.0 - purrr (version) ## Usage Philosophy package follows principles: 1. **Push Computations Database**: aggregations happen database,    minimizing data transfer memory usage. 2. **Familiar dplyr Syntax**: Uses standard dplyr/tidyverse patterns, making    easy integrate existing workflows. 3. **Lazy Evaluation**: Leverages dplyr's lazy evaluation build efficient    database queries. 4. **ggplot2 Output**: Plot functions return ggplot2 objects can    customized using standard ggplot2 functions. ## Breaking Changes 0.4.0 Version 0.4.0 introduced breaking changes: - **longer exports `   load magrittr explicitly `library(magrittr)`. - **Minimum R version increased**: Now requires R >= 4.1.0 (previously >= 3.1). - **Modern dependencies**: Updated require dplyr >= 1.0.0, rlang >= 1.0.0,   modern package versions.

title: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
text: Maintainer: Edgar Ruiz edgararuiz@gmail.com

title: dbplot: Simplifies Plotting Data Inside Databases — dbplot-package
code: # \dontrun{ library(dplyr) library(dbplot) library(DBI)  # Connect to database con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_mtcars <- copy_to(con, mtcars, "mtcars")  # Create histogram db_mtcars |>   dbplot_histogram(mpg)   # Create bar plot with custom aggregation db_mtcars |>   dbplot_bar(cyl, avg_mpg = mean(mpg))   # Get computation results for custom plotting db_mtcars |>   db_compute_bins(mpg, bins = 20) |>   ggplot2::ggplot() +   ggplot2::geom_col(ggplot2::aes(mpg, count))   # Use db_bin() directly in dplyr db_mtcars |>   group_by(bin = !!db_bin(mpg, bins = 10)) |>   count() #> # Source:   SQL [?? x 2] #> # Database: DuckDB 1.4.4 [unknown@Linux 6.14.0-1017-azure:R 4.5.3/:memory:] #> # Groups:   bin #>      bin     n #>    <dbl> <dbl> #>  1  22.2     3 #>  2  29.2     2 #>  3  12.8     4 #>  4  31.6     2 #>  5  17.5     6 #>  6  15.1     6 #>  7  10.4     2 #>  8  26.8     1 #>  9  24.5     1 #> 10  19.8     5  dbDisconnect(con) # }

title: Bar plot — dbplot_bar

title: Bar plot — dbplot_bar
code: dbplot_bar(data, x, ..., y = n())

text: data table (tbl) x discrete variable ... set named unnamed aggregations y aggregation formula. Defaults count (n)

text: ggplot object bar plot. multiple aggregations provided,   returns list ggplot objects, one aggregation.

title: Bar plot — dbplot_bar
code: # \dontrun{ library(DBI) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") db_mtcars <- copy_to(con, mtcars, "mtcars")  # Returns a plot of the row count per am db_mtcars |>   dbplot_bar(am)   # Returns a plot of the average mpg per am db_mtcars |>   dbplot_bar(am, mean(mpg))   # Returns the average and sum of mpg per am db_mtcars |>   dbplot_bar(am, avg_mpg = mean(mpg), sum_mpg = sum(mpg)) #> $avg_mpg  #>  #> $sum_mpg  #>   dbDisconnect(con) # }

title: Raster plot — dbplot_raster
text: visualize two continuous variables, typically resort Scatter plot. However, may practical visualizing millions billions dots representing intersections two variables. Raster plot may better option, concentrates intersections squares easier parse visually. title: dbplot 0.4.0
text: Package longer re-exports %>% pipe operator. Users use R’s native |> pipe (available R >= 4.1.0) load magrittr explicitly prefer %>%. Minimum R version increased 3.1 4.1.0

title: dbplot 0.4.0
text: dplyr >= 1.0.0 (>= 0.7) rlang >= 1.0.0 (>= 0.3) ggplot2 >= 3.3.0 (unversioned) dbplyr >= 2.0.0 (>= 1.4.0) testthat >= 3.0.0 (unversioned) Removed magrittr dependency (using native pipe) Migrated CI Travis CI GitHub Actions multi-platform testing (macOS, Windows, Ubuntu R devel/release/oldrel) Added automated test coverage reporting via codecov Added pkgdown site deployment automation

title: dbplot 0.4.0
text: examples now use native pipe |> instead %>% Fixed typos code documentation Improved S3 method exports using modern roxygen2 patterns Consolidated globalVariables() declarations better maintainability Added input validation bins, binwidth, resolution parameters Modernized test suite use testthat 3e patterns

title: dbplot 0.4.0
text: Examples now use DuckDB instead SQLite better performance boxplot support. DuckDB’s native quantile() function enables complete database workflow demonstrations including boxplot examples Added comprehensive package-level documentation Updated URLs current Posit documentation sites Added CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md CONTRIBUTING.md Enhanced pkgdown site Bootstrap 5 theme improved organization

title: dbplot 0.3.3
text: CRAN release: 2020-02-07 Exports pipe operator Adds missing examples Example README now uses SQLite (#17) Adds support vars() x argument db_compute_boxplot() (#27) Expands support db_compute_boxplot dbplyr backends (#23 @mkirzon) db_compute_boxplot can now return boxplots tbl objects existing grouping (eg useful facetted boxplots) (#23 @mkirzon)

title: dbplot 0.3.2
text: CRAN release: 2019-07-02 Addresses issue 'symbol' subsettable (#24)

title: dbplot 0.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2019-03-03 New db_compute_raster2() function includes upper limit Removes dependencies pipes Improves compliance rlangs quo() vs expr() usage rules Separates Spark default behaivor db_compute_boxplot() adds tests

title: dbplot 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2018-05-06 Supports multiple aggregations bar line charts Supports naming aggregations bar line charts
